title: schmaltzia trilobata var. quinata
intro: Schmaltzia trilobata var. quinata, also known as the five-lobed schmaltzia or trilobed schmaltzia, is a plant species within the family Burseraceae, native to tropical regions in Central and South America. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, and its medicinal properties are attributed to its resinous sap, which contains compounds such as terpenes and flavonoids. The health benefits of Schmaltzia trilobata var. quinata include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of Schmaltzia trilobata var. quinata often involve the use of its resinous sap, which can be extracted and incorporated into topical creams, ointments, or teas to harness its medicinal properties.
